title: The Vanished Price List
statement:
Load the page on the right. The heading is there. Nothing else is.

The prices, the workshop photo, the opening hours — they're all in the code. They're not on the screen.

Nobody deleted them.
constraints: Nothing gets deleted — the whole code stays
The note about the prices stays a note
After the fix you can see: the heading, both prices, the photo and the opening hours

checks.t6: The page says what language it's in

why.empty: The editor is empty. Deleting is not fixing. Bring the code back.
why.untouched: The code is exactly the one I gave you. You haven't changed anything.
why.not-closed: Some tag is opened and never closed, or closed in the wrong order.
why.lost-content: The heading is gone. The task is to bring back what vanished, not to delete more.
why.commented-out:
The content isn't on the page.

It's in the code — you can read it. But to the browser it doesn't exist.

Commented-out code doesn't exist. Between the opening and closing of a comment there is nothing, however much is written inside.

And a comment with no closing doesn't end anywhere. It runs to the end of the file, swallowing everything on the way.
why.lost-alt: The image is there, but its description vanished or is empty. That wasn't the mistake — leave it alone.
why.lost-head: Something vanished from the head of the page — the name, the language or the alphabet. Those weren't the mistake.

commented-out: Read the code top to bottom and find where the vanishing starts.
Just above the first missing price there's a line that opens something. Where does it close?
The comment was meant to end after the words about the prices. It doesn't end there — which is why it swallows them, and everything below.
lost-content: The heading wasn't part of the problem. Bring it back.

solution.why:
The first instinct, when half a page is missing, is to look for what got deleted. Nothing got deleted — the code is all there, line by line, and you can read every word of it.

That's what makes it hard to find: your eyes pass over the prices and the photo, see them, and your brain accepts that they're on the page.

But between the opening and closing of a comment, nothing exists. And this comment closes nowhere — so it ends nowhere. It runs to the end of the file and swallows everything after it.

The comment isn't the mistake. The missing end is.
steps: Load the page and see how far the visible part goes. The heading shows; the next line doesn't.
Look at the code right at that boundary. What sits between the last visible thing and the first missing one?
There's an open comment there. Check where it closes.
It doesn't close anywhere. Close it right after the note about the prices.
Load it again. Everything comes back at once.
solution.code:
Dobrich Bike Repair

Tyre change: 8 leva

Full service: 35 leva

Open Monday to Friday.
solution.note:
One missing end, and three lines of content plus an image disappear. No error, no warning, nothing red anywhere.

That's why a comment is handy when you want to switch something off temporarily — and a trap when you forget to close it.
